bio.education: University of Central Oklahoma, Theatre Arts and Design Technology
bio.biography:
Quick Facts
  • Parents are Kelly and Joe Zummo
  • Sisters Hailey and Megan Zummo played Division I lacrosse for Saint Mary’s College of California
  • Born with Fibular Hemimelia and Rroximal Femoral Focal Deficiency, two congenital disorders, causing her right foot to be amputated at 2 years old
  • Began playing standing volleyball in 2005 at Fallon Middle School
  • Married Brazilian sitting volleyball player Fabricio Da Silva Pinto in 2021
  • Has a dog named Athena, a German short-haired pointer and Labrador mix
  • Hobbies include sewing, weaving and painting
  • Hopes to open her own clothing business to create customized clothing and art after her volleyball career
  • Made and sold masks during the COVID pandemic in 2020
  • Named Best Libero of 2018 World ParaVolley Championships
  • Member of the U.S. Women's Sitting Team that was named Team USA's 2016 "Best Paralympic Team."
Paralympic Experience
  • 3-time Paralympian; 3-time Paralympic medalist (3 gold)
    • Paralympic Games Paris 2024, gold (Team - Women)
    • Paralympic Games Tokyo 2020, gold (Team - Women)
    • Paralympic Games Rio de Janeiro 2016, gold (Team - Women)
World Championship Experience
  • Most recent: 2022 – bronze (Team - Women)
  • Years of participation: Team - Women 2014, 2018, 2022
  • Medals: 3 (2 silver, 1 bronze)
    • Silver – 2018 (Team - Women); 2014 (Team - Women)
    • Bronze – 2022 (Team - Women)
More
USA Volleyball

Quick Facts
  • Position: Forward
  • Daughter of Mike and Lori Zumwinkle
  • Has two sisters, Anna and Emily
  • Two-time Patty Kazmaier Award finalist, given to the top player in women’s college hockey
Biographical Information 

Grace Zumwinkle grew up in Minnesota and decided to try hockey in kindergarten after one of her friends brought a pamphlet about the sport to school. Zumwinkle would excel at hockey and other sports like golf and tennis in high school, and attended the University of Minnesota to play college hockey, where she finished her college career with 109 goals, becoming the sixth Gopher in program history to score 100 career goals.

Zumwinkle made her IIHF World Championship debut at the 2021 IIHF World Championships and made her Olympic debut the following year for the U.S. women’s hockey team at the Olympic Winter Games Beijing 2022, where they won a silver medal. 

Zumwinkle was drafted in the third round (13th overall) in the 2023 PWHL Draft and has spent her entire PWHL career with the Minnesota Frost. She is a two-time Walter Cup champion with Minnesota.

Olympic Experience
  • 2-time Olympian; 2-time Olympic medalist (1 gold, 1 silver)
    • Olympic Games Milan 2026, gold (Team - Women)
    • Olympic Games Beijing 2022, silver (Team - Women)
World Championships Experience
  • Most recent: 2025 – gold (Team - Women)
  • Years of participation: Team - Women 2021, 2022, 2025
  • Medals: 3 (1 gold, 2 silver)
    • Gold – 2025 (Team - Women)
    • Silver – 2022 (Team - Women); 2021 (Team - Women)

bio.education: University of Wisconsin-Whitewater ’20, University of Alabama
bio.biography:
Quick Facts
  • Daughter of Chris and Laura Zurbrugg
  • Has one brother, Collin
  • Was paralyzed after attending a basketball camp where she injured her back, exacerbating a medical condition called “tethered spinal cord” that she was unknowingly born with
  • Loves beekeeping with her dad and cooking food with her mom
  • Has over 130 pairs of long socks with everything from Wonder Woman to bacon socks
  • Loves Star Wars
  • Aspires to be an athletic trainer for Paralympic athletes.
Paralympic Experience
  • 2-time Paralympian; 2-time Paralympic medalist (1 silver, 1 bronze)
    • Paralympic Games Paris 2024, silver (Team - Women)
    • Paralympic Games Tokyo 2020, bronze (Team - Women)
World Championships Experience
  • Most recent: 2023 – bronze (Team - Women)
  • Years of participation: Team - Women 2018, 2023
  • Medals: 1 (1 bronze)
    • Bronze – 2023 (Team - Women)

bio.education: Sun Prairie High School (Sun Prairie, Wisconsin) '17 College of Saint Scholastica '21
bio.biography:
Quick Facts
  • In 2011 she was diagnosed with Stargardt disease, a degenerative eye condition resulting in a loss of central vision.
  • Daughter of Michael and Jennifer Zutter
  • Has one sister, Natalie
  • Hobbies include hiking, figure skating, swimming and photography.
Paralympic Experience
  • 1-time Paralympian
    • Paralympic Games Pyeongchang 2018, 8th (15km Freestyle - Visually Impaired - Women), 9th (1km Sprint Classic Style - Visually Impaired - Wom, 7.5km Classic Style - Visually Impaired - Women), 12th (4 x 2.5km Relay Open - Open)

USA Hockey

Quick Facts
  • De Lavalette is the most critically injured survivor of the Brussels Airport terrorist bombing on March 22, 2016
  • She was 17 years old at the time of the attack
  • A rider from the age of three, Beatrice has never given up
  • Five months after suffering massive internal injuries, second and third degree burns, a spinal cord injury, and the amputation of both lower legs, she was back on her beloved mare, Delegada X
  • Her courage and grit helped her return to school in September 2016, compete in her first para-equestrian show in April 2017, and graduate on time with her class in June 2017
  • Shares her incredible story as a motivational speaker
  • In January 2019, she was a featured speaker at the Equestrian Businesswomen Summit in West Palm Beach, Fla
  • She is a student at the University of San Diego and trains with Shayna Simon Dressage at Shannon and Steffen Peters’ Arroyo Del Mar
  • Wants to start a company that makes affordable adaptive sports equipment.
Paralympic Experience
  • 1-time Paralympian
    • Paralympic Games Tokyo 2020, 5th (Individual Championship Test - Grade II - Open), 6th (Individual Freestyle - Grade II - Open)
